headline: AI Hits an Inflection Point: Agents Go Live, Infrastructure Breaks, Morgan Stanley Warns Everyone's Not Ready

stories.title: Morgan Stanley Warns AI Breakthrough Coming in H1 2026—Infrastructure Crunch Is Real
stories.summary: Morgan Stanley's report signals a massive AI leap incoming this year, but warns of a brutal power shortage hitting US grids (9-18 GW deficit through 2028). Companies are already converting Bitcoin mines to data centers and deploying fuel cells to stay ahead.
stories.takeaway: The real bottleneck isn't AI capability—it's electricity. Infrastructure constraints will reshape where AI actually gets deployed and who wins the compute race.

stories.title: McKinsey QuantumBlack: AI Adoption Challenges Are Experiential, Not Technical
stories.summary: New McKinsey research drops the hard truth: most orgs are stuck in pilot hell because they can't get people to actually use AI tools, not because the tech is broken. Adoption challenges are about user buy-in and workflow redesign.
stories.takeaway: Scaling AI isn't a tech problem—it's a people problem. Teams that nail change management and user experience will outpace those betting on pure tech improvements.

stories.title: OpenClaw Becomes GitHub's Most-Starred Project Ever, OpenAI Acqui-Hires Creator
stories.summary: OpenClaw went from one developer's side project to 68k GitHub stars in weeks, surpassing React and Linux. OpenAI brought the creator onboard in Feb, hinting at a consumer-friendly version coming soon.
stories.takeaway: Agentic AI finally has a viral moment. When OpenAI polishes this tool, expect rapid mainstream adoption of AI agents that actually execute tasks.

stories.title: CB Insights: Multimodal AI Agents Winning Customer Service Land Grab in 2026
stories.summary: Voice AI is the proving ground—high costs, zero tolerance for latency failures. Companies deploying agents at scale need multimodal capabilities (voice + text + images) to win enterprise deals. Observability tooling is becoming the bottleneck.
stories.takeaway: As agents move to production, the real margin shift moves from building agents to deploying, securing, and monitoring them. Observability is the next M&A battleground.

watchNext.title: MIT Technology Review: Bridging the Operational AI Gap—Agentic AI Moving to Production
watchNext.why: Deep dive on why 40% of agentic AI projects will get cancelled by 2027. Gartner's warning hits hard—it's not about capability, it's about integration infrastructure and governance models.

watchNext.title: Benedict Evans: AI Eats the World Keynote (B2BMX 2026)—AI as Platform Shift, Not Magic Wand
watchNext.why: Evans cuts through the hype, contextualizing AI against past tech shifts (mainframes → PCs → smartphones). Big Four spent $400B on AI in 2025, budgeted $650B for 2026. Game-changing perspective on realistic AI adoption timelines.
